name: WRF
description: The Weather Research and Forecasting (WRF) Model is a next-generation mesoscale numerical weather prediction system designed for atmospheric research and operational forecasting. It supports a wide range of meteorological applications including real-data simulations, idealized cases, data assimilation (WRF-DA/WRFDA), atmospheric chemistry (WRF-Chem), hydrology (WRF-Hydro), and adjoint/tangent-linear modeling (WRFPLUS/WRFTLADJ). WRF features multiple dynamical cores (ARW - Advanced Research WRF, and NMM - Nonhydrostatic Mesoscale Model), multiple physics options for microphysics, cumulus parameterization, planetary boundary layer, land-surface models, radiation, and surface layer schemes. It supports MPI-based parallel computing, nested domains, multiple I/O formats (NetCDF, GRIB1/GRIB2, HDF5, ADIOS2), and can be coupled with ocean models via ESMF/OASIS3. The model is developed and maintained by NCAR/UCAR and is widely used by the meteorological research community worldwide.
github_description: The official repository for the Weather Research and Forecasting (WRF) model
